topic: Müller, K., & Zhang, L. (2023). Quantifying confabulation in autoregressive language models: The confabulation coefficient and cascade effects. Journal of Artificial Intelligence Research, 78(4), 1123–1145. doi:10.48550/arXiv.2305.12345
final_review:
The concept of confabulation in autoregressive language models has gained significant attention in recent years, with researchers exploring its implications and potential applications (Müller & Zhang, 2023). In the context of language models, confabulation refers to the generation of false or misleading information, which can have serious consequences in various domains, including healthcare and scientific writing (Vipula Rawte et al., 2023; Edisa Lozić et al., 2023). 

Research on confabulation has been conducted in various fields, including neuroscience, where studies have investigated the neural mechanisms underlying memory retrieval and the potential for confabulation (Asaf Gilboa et al., 2009; Gerhard Werner, 2010). For instance, Asaf Gilboa et al. (2009) found that lesions in the ventromedial prefrontal cortex can produce early functional alterations during remote memory retrieval, which can lead to confabulation. Similarly, Gerhard Werner (2010) discussed the conceptual implications of fractals in the nervous system for theoretical neuroscience, highlighting the complexity of neural processes.

The application of large language models in healthcare has also been explored, with researchers developing frameworks for human evaluation of these models (Thomas Yu Chow Tam et al., 2024). The role of belief in occurrence within autobiographical memory has also been investigated, with researchers exploring the relationship between belief and memory retrieval (Alan Scoboria et al., 2013). This research has implications for our understanding of confabulation and its underlying mechanisms. Moreover, the NIA-AA Research Framework has provided a biological definition of Alzheimer's disease, which can inform research on confabulation and its relationship to neurological disorders (Jack et al., 2018).

In conclusion, the study of confabulation in autoregressive language models is a complex and multidisciplinary field, with implications for various domains, including healthcare, scientific writing, and neuroscience. Further research is needed to fully understand the mechanisms underlying confabulation and to develop effective strategies for mitigating its negative consequences.
